narration: You signed up with good intentions — probably January first, maybe after a rough week. Here's the thing: the gym was counting on you to quit.
visual: Gym membership card SVG springs in on dark background, $58/MONTH text on card, bar chart animates showing visit frequency — spike in JAN then drops to near-zero by JUN

narration: Sixty-seven percent of gym memberships are completely unused. That's two out of three people paying month after month for a gym they literally never visit.
visual: Large '67%' counter animating from 0 on light background, amber progress bar fills to 67%, three person silhouettes — two grayed out one amber, '2 OUT OF 3 PEOPLE' badge springs in

narration: This isn't an accident — it's the business model. Planet Fitness packs 6,500 members into gyms that hold 300. They literally can't serve everyone, and they don't want to.
visual: Gym building SVG with columns and 'GYM' sign on dark background, crowd of 12 person silhouettes crowded inside, '6,500 MEMBERS vs 300 CAPACITY' stats reveal

narration: It's called purchase intention bias. Buying the membership feels like working out — your brain gets a dopamine hit from just signing up, so you never actually go.
visual: Brain SVG on light background with dumbbell thought bubble, satisfaction meter fills to 100% just from the membership card, 'BUYING IT = DOING IT (TO YOUR BRAIN)' fades in

narration: The average gym membership runs $696 a year. Americans waste 1.8 billion dollars every year on gyms they never use — and you've probably been part of that number.
visual: $696 counter animating up on dark background, progress bar fills, '$1.8 BILLION WASTED' in red fades in below, 'ON GYMS THEY NEVER USE' badge springs in

narration: Here's your move: count how many times you've been in the last 30 days. Under four? Cancel today and redirect that $58 somewhere that actually works for you.
visual: Calendar SVG with 4 green checkmarks on light background, piggy bank with $58 label beside it, 'UNDER 4 VISITS? CANCEL TODAY.' text, dark CTA card 'REDIRECT THAT $58 / INTO SAVINGS INSTEAD'
